commands.review_summary:
The branch adds AgentHarness tool-name validation before constructor and setTools registration, plus regression tests for malformed and unreadable tool names.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +96. Total +118 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main reads `tool.name` directly in the AgentHarness constructor and setTools paths, so non-string or throwing name descriptors can escape the intended harness error boundary; the added tests exercise those paths directly.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to choose the intended harness registry policy and coordinate the open sibling PR; there is no narrow automated repair to make on this branch.

target.title: fix(agent-core): validate harness tool names
target.body:
## Summary
- Validate AgentHarness tool names before constructor registration and `setTools()` replacement.
- Convert malformed/non-string/empty names and unreadable name accessors into `AgentHarnessError("invalid_argument", ...)`.
- Add regression coverage proving failed replacement registration does not mutate the existing harness tool set.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox `run_d8fec9fabf2b`, lease `cbx_f6cb608a7425`: `env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `AgentHarness` no longer accepts malformed tool names or leaks raw name-accessor errors while registering constructor tools or replacing tools with `setTools()`.

Real environment tested: local focused agent-core harness tests on this branch, plus Linux AWS Crabbox changed gate on provider `aws` lease `cbx_f6cb608a7425`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.ts packages/agent-core/src/harness/agent-harness.test.ts`; AWS Crabbox `env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 tests; targeted formatter/lint/diff checks passed on final head `a711fe355ae`; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on final head; AWS Crabbox changed gate selected `core` and `coreTests` and exited 0 before the final fast-forward rebase.

Observed result after fix: malformed harness tool names fail closed with `AgentHarnessError` before active tool state is poisoned, and a failed `setTools()` replacement leaves the previous harness usable.

What was not tested: a full live assistant turn with a deliberately malformed app-supplied `AgentTool`; the unit coverage exercises the harness registration and replacement paths directly.
target.author: vincentkoc
